name: Madden, Arthur Alexander
motto: "For blessing ever waits on virtuous deeds, And though a late, a sure reward succeeds."
clubs: Reporter and Chairman Program Committee Alpha Phi Epsilon, '23
description: One of the mainstays of everything literary on the campus; Hermaean, Alpha Phi Epsilon, the Scribblers and the Mississippian owe him much. A very likeable kind of chap—doesn't look a bit intelligent on first acquaintance—he's the kind Ole Miss hates to lose.
text:
"For blessing ever waits on virtuous deeds, And though a late, a sure reward succeeds."
One of the mainstays of everything literary on the campus; Hermaean, Alpha Phi Epsilon, the Scribblers and the Mississippian owe him much. A very likeable kind of chap—doesn't look a bit intelligent on first acquaintance—he's the kind Ole Miss hates to lose.

name: Markette, Mabel Lynne
motto: "A miniature of loveliness, all grace Summed up and closed in, little."
clubs: Secretary of A. S. B. O., and Ole Misses
description: No one is better known at Ole Miss than "Babe." This year she is the most popular of the "Co-eds." Her winsome smile has won the hearts of both "Eds" and "Co-eds"; the Profs, also, seem to fall, for her scholastic standing is equal to her sociability.
text:
"A miniature of loveliness, all grace Summed up and closed in, little."
No one is better known at Ole Miss than "Babe." This year she is the most popular of the "Co-eds." Her winsome smile has won the hearts of both "Eds" and "Co-eds"; the Profs, also, seem to fall, for her scholastic standing is equal to her sociability.

name: Alexander, George Edward
motto: "I dare do all that may become a man Who dares do more is none."
description: Here you see one of the hardest fighting guards Ole Miss has produced. "Cy" isn't exactly brilliant, but he's a hard worker and "peach of a fellow."
text:
"I dare do all that may become a man Who dares do more is none."
Here you see one of the hardest fighting guards Ole Miss has produced. "Cy" isn't exactly brilliant, but he's a hard worker and "peach of a fellow."

name: Stevenson, William Cameron
motto: "No profit grows where is no pleasure ta'en."
description: "Cam" is a doubtful case, it is easily possible that he's a good student, perhaps—but as a charmer of women he has no rival. Though his mind be lacking in other information, he can glibly quote the railroad schedules between Holly Springs and Corinth.
text:
"No profit grows where is no pleasure ta'en."
"Cam" is a doubtful case, it is easily possible that he's a good student, perhaps—but as a charmer of women he has no rival. Though his mind be lacking in other information, he can glibly quote the railroad schedules between Holly Springs and Corinth.

name: Pettis, Levi McCulloch
motto: "True wit is nature to advantage dressed, What oft was thought, but ne'er so well expressed."
description: "Zip" is the fellow who will tell you the "hard truth" and emphasize it with a sweep of the digits. He has little to say in a crowd, but—those who saw him in the Marionettes "Stop Thief," think his line is law and not preaching. Good Mr. "Pettis," how we love him!
text:
"True wit is nature to advantage dressed, What oft was thought, but ne'er so well expressed."
"Zip" is the fellow who will tell you the "hard truth" and emphasize it with a sweep of the digits. He has little to say in a crowd, but—those who saw him in the Marionettes "Stop Thief," think his line is law and not preaching. Good Mr. "Pettis," how we love him!

name: Woodruff, Fannie Mae
motto: "True as the needle to the pole, or as the dial to the sun."
description: Fannie Mae has a camouflage of a quiet, exclusive manner, which encloses the real individual. The ideals for which she stands are always the highest. No jollier comrade nor truer friend can be found.
text:
"True as the needle to the pole, or as the dial to the sun."
Fannie Mae has a camouflage of a quiet, exclusive manner, which encloses the real individual. The ideals for which she stands are always the highest. No jollier comrade nor truer friend can be found.

name: Davis, Curtis Gray
motto: "What's mine is yours, and what's yours is mine."
description: Every man has his specialty, and we think that Davis' is above all else, a never failing dependability. A cheerful comrade, and a dependable friend, his departure leaves a vacancy hard to fill.
text:
"What's mine is yours, and what's yours is mine."
Every man has his specialty, and we think that Davis' is above all else, a never failing dependability. A cheerful comrade, and a dependable friend, his departure leaves a vacancy hard to fill.

name: Taylor, William Murray
motto: "Affliction may one day smile again; And 'till then sit thee down, sorrow!"
description: "Guy" started in Engineering, in which he made a fine record—but he felt the call to the ranks of the ministry, and this year he gets a lit degree. Likable, and of good disposition, he is sure of a beneficent career.
text:
"Affliction may one day smile again; And 'till then sit thee down, sorrow!"
"Guy" started in Engineering, in which he made a fine record—but he felt the call to the ranks of the ministry, and this year he gets a lit degree. Likable, and of good disposition, he is sure of a beneficent career.

name: Beckett, M. Howorth
motto: "Give to the world the best that you have, and the best will come back to you."
clubs: National Committee-man of Alpha Phi Epsilon, '22
description: A man of sterling worth and certain success. Beckett has filled more responsible positions and put across more big things than perhaps any other one man. When he is gone, another name will be added to those of our leaders who have "gone on," and in a few years another name will be added to those of "our great alumni."
text:
"Give to the world the best that you have, and the best will come back to you."
A man of sterling worth and certain success. Beckett has filled more responsible positions and put across more big things than perhaps any other one man. When he is gone, another name will be added to those of our leaders who have "gone on," and in a few years another name will be added to those of "our great alumni."

name: Tyner, Furman Hillman
motto: "All his faults are such that one loves him still the better for them."
description: Furman distinguished himself last spring at the royal profession of pitching washers—and it is whispered that he is able to maintain his supremacy at the council table, where worship is paid to the supreme god—"Golden Bull." He is liked by all, and a favorite with his associates—nothing but success can come to him.
text:
"All his faults are such that one loves him still the better for them."
Furman distinguished himself last spring at the royal profession of pitching washers—and it is whispered that he is able to maintain his supremacy at the council table, where worship is paid to the supreme god—"Golden Bull." He is liked by all, and a favorite with his associates—nothing but success can come to him.

name: Wilson, Braswell
motto: "There was no room in him to hold the memory of wrong."
description: Red-headed, without spectacles, and just the same kind of face as his brother. Red finishes in medicine this year with a good record. His popularity is attested by his being chosen as secretary-treasurer of the Med Club. Another good man leaves us to join the struggle for existence.
text:
"There was no room in him to hold the memory of wrong."
Red-headed, without spectacles, and just the same kind of face as his brother. Red finishes in medicine this year with a good record. His popularity is attested by his being chosen as secretary-treasurer of the Med Club. Another good man leaves us to join the struggle for existence.

name: Mitchell, Theodore Curtis
motto: "Life is a jest, and all things show it; I thought so once, but now I know it."
description: Theodore is sometimes considered a handsome boy, when he hasn't a big black cigar in his mouth, which is seldom. He is really a "medical curiosity," being without a heart of his own—it is whispered that he lost it this year to one of our fair co-eds.
text:
"Life is a jest, and all things show it; I thought so once, but now I know it."
Theodore is sometimes considered a handsome boy, when he hasn't a big black cigar in his mouth, which is seldom. He is really a "medical curiosity," being without a heart of his own—it is whispered that he lost it this year to one of our fair co-eds.

name: Galloway, Dolph Vernon
motto: "The voice so sweet, the words so fair, As some soft chime had stroked the air."
description: "Red" has the voice of a lamb, but the courage of a lion. That falsetto voice so characteristic of him, can be as shrill as a siren whistle when anyone dares to dispute. A better student, everything considered, is yet to enter Ole Miss.
text:
"The voice so sweet, the words so fair, As some soft chime had stroked the air."
"Red" has the voice of a lamb, but the courage of a lion. That falsetto voice so characteristic of him, can be as shrill as a siren whistle when anyone dares to dispute. A better student, everything considered, is yet to enter Ole Miss.

name: Cocchiara, Peter Paul
motto: "He cast off his friends as a huntsman his pack."
description: Peter is the wonder boy of New Orleans; he is an authority on all questions. Perhaps because they wouldn't feed him enough, "Kosherry" left Tulane for Ole Miss, where he found his true field. We shall always uphold him as the champion skid consumer of the world. He is one of the most enterprising of the Meds, and great things are hoped of him in the future.
text:
"He cast off his friends as a huntsman his pack."
Peter is the wonder boy of New Orleans; he is an authority on all questions. Perhaps because they wouldn't feed him enough, "Kosherry" left Tulane for Ole Miss, where he found his true field. We shall always uphold him as the champion skid consumer of the world. He is one of the most enterprising of the Meds, and great things are hoped of him in the future.

name: Bailey, Sanford Lamar
motto: "Ye little Stars! hide your diminished ray."
description: Though Sanford has made a fine sporting editor for the Mississippian, and notwithstanding the fact that he daily surprises his instructors by his ---- (problem: what does the blank mean?), his most glaring success was as reporter of the French Club. Bailey is the life of any party, and as his sweater has made him widely known, there is hardly a better liked man on the campus.
text:
"Ye little Stars! hide your diminished ray."
Though Sanford has made a fine sporting editor for the Mississippian, and notwithstanding the fact that he daily surprises his instructors by his ---- (problem: what does the blank mean?), his most glaring success was as reporter of the French Club. Bailey is the life of any party, and as his sweater has made him widely known, there is hardly a better liked man on the campus.

name: Arrington, George Lamar
motto: "I find the medicine worse than the malady."
description: "Skinny" is known to all as the great "hope in life" of the Senior Meds. A good student, and a good fellow; we wish him the best of luck in his profession.
text:
"I find the medicine worse than the malady."
"Skinny" is known to all as the great "hope in life" of the Senior Meds. A good student, and a good fellow; we wish him the best of luck in his profession.

name: Murray Smith, Causey
motto: "Why should a man, whose blood is warm within, Sit like his grandsire cut in alabaster?"
description: "Moderator" does not talk very fast, and not very much, but what he says is usually as humorous as dry wit can be. A more constant friend of those who know him best, never lived. He has played some Scrub Football and was in several class games, but his work kept him from putting in the time necessary to make Varsity. His grades in the Medical Department will indicate his ability.
text:
"Why should a man, whose blood is warm within, Sit like his grandsire cut in alabaster?"
"Moderator" does not talk very fast, and not very much, but what he says is usually as humorous as dry wit can be. A more constant friend of those who know him best, never lived. He has played some Scrub Football and was in several class games, but his work kept him from putting in the time necessary to make Varsity. His grades in the Medical Department will indicate his ability.

name: Sandie Raymond, King
motto: "For e'en though vanquished, he could argue still."
clubs: National Chairman of L. O. M. R.
Organizer of Tri-State Chairman Soldiers-Sailors' Relief Club, '18, '19
Member of Disabled Veterans of World War
Member of American Civil Liberties Union
description: "Sadie" has ideas all his own, and we dare not say that any of them are wrong, perhaps we have not reached the stage in mental development that will allow us to grasp theories that are the very passion of his life. Dreamers have always been the forerunners of great changes for the good of humanity. Can we say that King is not a Rousseau, a Luther, or a Marx?
text:
"For e'en though vanquished, he could argue still."
"Sadie" has ideas all his own, and we dare not say that any of them are wrong, perhaps we have not reached the stage in mental development that will allow us to grasp theories that are the very passion of his life. Dreamers have always been the forerunners of great changes for the good of humanity. Can we say that King is not a Rousseau, a Luther, or a Marx?

name: Clinton Howard, Williams
motto: "My library was dukedom large enough."
clubs: Member of Y. M. C. A. Council, '20
description: A man of deep convictions, an orator with a silver tongue, a student of no mean ability, a quiet, yet an altogether likeable fellow. Hermaean could hardly exist without his presence.
text:
"My library was dukedom large enough."
A man of deep convictions, an orator with a silver tongue, a student of no mean ability, a quiet, yet an altogether likeable fellow. Hermaean could hardly exist without his presence.

name: Frank, Clark
motto: "Wherever the bright sun of heaven shall shine, His honor and the greatness of his name Shall be, and make new nations."
description: "Ked" Clark knows the law and makes the fellows in practice court step lively to win their cases, only an indicator of what he will do as a real attorney. It would be trite to say, "a good student." We fear he has quite a few political aspirations.
text:
"Wherever the bright sun of heaven shall shine, His honor and the greatness of his name Shall be, and make new nations."
"Ked" Clark knows the law and makes the fellows in practice court step lively to win their cases, only an indicator of what he will do as a real attorney. It would be trite to say, "a good student." We fear he has quite a few political aspirations.

name: Oakley, Thomas Denton
motto: "Sleep shall neither night nor day Hang upon his pent-house lid."
description: One of the hardest workers on the campus, one who has made a brilliant record, for which he deserves added credit, for it is the result of real labor, rather than natural brilliance. "Oaks" is misunderstood by many, but those who know him well, know that beneath his brusque, unpolished manner, and lack of tact, that he has a heart of gold and they value his friendship highly.
text:
"Sleep shall neither night nor day Hang upon his pent-house lid."
One of the hardest workers on the campus, one who has made a brilliant record, for which he deserves added credit, for it is the result of real labor, rather than natural brilliance. "Oaks" is misunderstood by many, but those who know him well, know that beneath his brusque, unpolished manner, and lack of tact, that he has a heart of gold and they value his friendship highly.

name: Outlaw, Arthur F.
motto: "A young man married is a man that's marr'd."
description: Outlaw has been out for a few years in the ranks of the profession, and he returns this year to get his degree. A mighty good fellow, a fine tennis player and a credit to the "American Society of Civil Engineers."
text:
"A young man married is a man that's marr'd."
Outlaw has been out for a few years in the ranks of the profession, and he returns this year to get his degree. A mighty good fellow, a fine tennis player and a credit to the "American Society of Civil Engineers."

name: Knight, Curtis C.
motto: "Give every man thine ear, but few, thy voice."
description: When he leaves Ole Miss, Curtis expects first to "roll the pills." Later, he expects to settle down and raise—well "featherless chickens." Here's luck to all his undertakings.
text:
"Give every man thine ear, but few, thy voice."
When he leaves Ole Miss, Curtis expects first to "roll the pills." Later, he expects to settle down and raise—well "featherless chickens." Here's luck to all his undertakings.

name: Thomas, M. M.
motto: "O, thou art fairer than the evening air Clad in the beauty of a thousand stars."
description: Melvin is a constant and inexhaustible source of delight at all the dances; he has won the hearts of all the girls with whom he has secured after-dance dates. We feel confident that he will be a notable success in pharmacy, his chosen profession.
text:
"O, thou art fairer than the evening air Clad in the beauty of a thousand stars."
Melvin is a constant and inexhaustible source of delight at all the dances; he has won the hearts of all the girls with whom he has secured after-dance dates. We feel confident that he will be a notable success in pharmacy, his chosen profession.

name: Cutcliff, W. H.
motto: "That soul that can be honest is the only perfect man."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer Senior Pharmacy Class
description: His activities are many and varied—"soda-jerker," baseball player, member of the firm of "Cutcliff and Holland, Importers, Inc." And, yes, he is also a student. As the only freshman to make the baseball "M" in '22, great things are expected of him this year. A fine all-round fellow. His loss will be sincerely felt by those he leaves behind.
text:
"That soul that can be honest is the only perfect man."
His activities are many and varied—"soda-jerker," baseball player, member of the firm of "Cutcliff and Holland, Importers, Inc." And, yes, he is also a student. As the only freshman to make the baseball "M" in '22, great things are expected of him this year. A fine all-round fellow. His loss will be sincerely felt by those he leaves behind.

name: Edwards, Gilbert Dewitt
motto: "A fertile brain; a calm and purposeful spirit."
description: One of the most unassuming and unpretentious men on the campus, and one of the best, he always has a good word for every one. In comparison with some of our "constitutional knockers," it is even soothing to listen to him. Edwards is a good student and is certain to succeed. We wish him the best of luck.
text:
"A fertile brain; a calm and purposeful spirit."
One of the most unassuming and unpretentious men on the campus, and one of the best, he always has a good word for every one. In comparison with some of our "constitutional knockers," it is even soothing to listen to him. Edwards is a good student and is certain to succeed. We wish him the best of luck.

name: Flowers, Taylor Graydon
motto: "If it be a gentleman and a scholar ye seek, ye have found him."
description: "Buck" has an attractive face, one you can't help but smile at—excuse me, I meant with—and can't help liking. Tall and rangy, he made one of the best outfielders last spring and his bat was able in every case to supply a needed hit. We wish him "God-speed" in all his undertakings.
text:
"If it be a gentleman and a scholar ye seek, ye have found him."
"Buck" has an attractive face, one you can't help but smile at—excuse me, I meant with—and can't help liking. Tall and rangy, he made one of the best outfielders last spring and his bat was able in every case to supply a needed hit. We wish him "God-speed" in all his undertakings.

name: Lester, Lionel E.
motto: "Open the old cigar box, get me a Cuba Stout."
description: "Red" was one of our most consistent pitchers last spring, and great things are hoped of him for the coming season. He has shown his industry and ability in the success he has made as football manager this year. May "Lady Luck" be with him throughout life is our wish.
text:
"Open the old cigar box, get me a Cuba Stout."
"Red" was one of our most consistent pitchers last spring, and great things are hoped of him for the coming season. He has shown his industry and ability in the success he has made as football manager this year. May "Lady Luck" be with him throughout life is our wish.

name: Gant, Jamie Didlake
motto: "Then did she lift her hand to his chin, And perceive the pretty dimples of the skin."
description: Jimmy's "Advice to the Lovelorn" and many formulae on "Vamping the Fair Sex" are unexcelled, and in the terpsichorean arts he is a delight to the fairer sex. Jimmy is well liked by his classmates, and his college life will prove to be only the beginning of success.
text:
"Then did she lift her hand to his chin, And perceive the pretty dimples of the skin."
Jimmy's "Advice to the Lovelorn" and many formulae on "Vamping the Fair Sex" are unexcelled, and in the terpsichorean arts he is a delight to the fairer sex. Jimmy is well liked by his classmates, and his college life will prove to be only the beginning of success.

name: Garnett, James M.
motto: "What is a man if he is not a mystery."
description: "Jim" has been a faithful student in the School of Pharmacy, but his most signal success has been in the formation of friendships. Many of us are there who will ever remember his true, manly qualities and his cheerful disposition.
text:
"What is a man if he is not a mystery."
"Jim" has been a faithful student in the School of Pharmacy, but his most signal success has been in the formation of friendships. Many of us are there who will ever remember his true, manly qualities and his cheerful disposition.

name: Cooper, Cleo Clayton
motto: "Tongue nor heart cannot conceive nor name thee!"
description: His ability to make friends, his sense of humor, and his keen insight coupled with his knowledge of Pharmacy, guarantees success for him in his chosen profession.
text:
"Tongue nor heart cannot conceive nor name thee!"
His ability to make friends, his sense of humor, and his keen insight coupled with his knowledge of Pharmacy, guarantees success for him in his chosen profession.

name: Day, Samuel Kirby
motto: "Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall not be disappointed."
description: "Kirk" comes to us from Tulane. To try to enumerate his good qualities would be only to elaborate on his approved abilities. Aside from his classroom work, he plays his part at Ricks. We wish you much success, Kirby, and hate to see you leave us.
text:
"Blessed is he who expects nothing, for he shall not be disappointed."
"Kirk" comes to us from Tulane. To try to enumerate his good qualities would be only to elaborate on his approved abilities. Aside from his classroom work, he plays his part at Ricks. We wish you much success, Kirby, and hate to see you leave us.

name: Holman, Clyde Bass II
motto: "Wise men say nothing in dangerous times."
clubs: Pi Kappa Phi, Gamma Iota Chapter
description: Bass was a prominent member of the football squad last year, and but for illness in mid-season he would have been a valuable man this year. Though we feel he is fit for better things (?) we know he will make a great success of his life-work. He leaves many friends here to regret his leaving.
text:
"Wise men say nothing in dangerous times."
Bass was a prominent member of the football squad last year, and but for illness in mid-season he would have been a valuable man this year. Though we feel he is fit for better things (?) we know he will make a great success of his life-work. He leaves many friends here to regret his leaving.

name: Booth, William Harper
motto: "I hold he loves me best that calls me 'Hot-Shot.'"
description: "Hot-Shot," the beautiful pride and joy of Guntown, is perhaps the most widely known dignitary on the campus. He achieved immediate renown upon the occasion of his first entering the campus. "Hot" assisted the baseball scrubs nobly, and it is thought by some that he may become a "pharmachute."
text:
"I hold he loves me best that calls me 'Hot-Shot.'"
"Hot-Shot," the beautiful pride and joy of Guntown, is perhaps the most widely known dignitary on the campus. He achieved immediate renown upon the occasion of his first entering the campus. "Hot" assisted the baseball scrubs nobly, and it is thought by some that he may become a "pharmachute."

name: Temple, Walter Newton
motto: "Thou say'st an undisputed thing In such a solemn way."
description: Walter has taken a prominent part in student activities this year, and has done credit to himself and to the school. He is a conscientious fellow and a loyal supporter of Ole Miss. One is almost tempted to say he was made for better things than pharmacy—er, Beg pardon!
text:
"Thou say'st an undisputed thing In such a solemn way."
Walter has taken a prominent part in student activities this year, and has done credit to himself and to the school. He is a conscientious fellow and a loyal supporter of Ole Miss. One is almost tempted to say he was made for better things than pharmacy—er, Beg pardon!

name: Wilson, Fuller F.
motto: "We grant, although he had much wit, He was very shy of using it."
description: As cunning as the fox, but never does he endeavor to put anything by his dear profs in a cunning manner. Many will be the sale of Wilson's Snake-oil, Corn Cure, the best on the market!
text:
"We grant, although he had much wit, He was very shy of using it."
As cunning as the fox, but never does he endeavor to put anything by his dear profs in a cunning manner. Many will be the sale of Wilson's Snake-oil, Corn Cure, the best on the market!

name: Cammack, Ernest Eugene
motto: "My company was not unacceptable to the young and careless as well as to the studious and literary."
description: "Kid's" greatest failing is his ever ending desire for knowledge that lies within the fund of books. At times, though, we see him strolling down the walk with a girl swinging on his arm.
text:
"My company was not unacceptable to the young and careless as well as to the studious and literary."
"Kid's" greatest failing is his ever ending desire for knowledge that lies within the fund of books. At times, though, we see him strolling down the walk with a girl swinging on his arm.

name: Robbins, Wood S.
motto: "Gather rosebuds while ye may Old time is still a-flying."
description: A fellow who is striving to do those things here that are to gauge his walk through life. Not melancholy, Oh! no, nor yet over-joyous, just a happy medium fellow, loved by those who know him best.
text:
"Gather rosebuds while ye may Old time is still a-flying."
A fellow who is striving to do those things here that are to gauge his walk through life. Not melancholy, Oh! no, nor yet over-joyous, just a happy medium fellow, loved by those who know him best.

name: Furr, James Francis
motto: "Let me not play a woman; I have a beard coming."
description: Furr is far from further! We never hear any noise out of him except his brain at work. A quiet, unassuming lad that has the making of a wonderful mixer of drugs for the woes of mankind.
text:
"Let me not play a woman; I have a beard coming."
Furr is far from further! We never hear any noise out of him except his brain at work. A quiet, unassuming lad that has the making of a wonderful mixer of drugs for the woes of mankind.

name: Tyrone, Victor Young
motto: "Though I am always in haste I am never in a hurry."
description: All compliments to the above photograph, for the owner was quite doubtful as to its representativeness - it is most difficult to properly portray the physiognomy of such a handsome man. "Vic" brought his best from his other schools to this, his Alma Mater.
text:
"Though I am always in haste I am never in a hurry."
All compliments to the above photograph, for the owner was quite doubtful as to its representativeness - it is most difficult to properly portray the physiognomy of such a handsome man. "Vic" brought his best from his other schools to this, his Alma Mater.

name: Tucker, Hal Shackelford
motto: "All your better deeds Shall be in water writ, but this in marble."
description: "Tuck" learned to drill in the S. A. T. C. and he continues to drill even now, but instead of a gun and a uniform, it is a laboratory apron and a pipette. Did you ever see him without that famous grin, sometimes called a smile?
text:
"All your better deeds Shall be in water writ, but this in marble."
"Tuck" learned to drill in the S. A. T. C. and he continues to drill even now, but instead of a gun and a uniform, it is a laboratory apron and a pipette. Did you ever see him without that famous grin, sometimes called a smile?

name: Taylor, Thomas B.
motto: "A cheerful disposition is a fund of ready capital."
description: "Tommie," with several of his classmates, has been taking a course in light dramatics during the year. Since Pharmacy, he will practice; he must know the whims of humanity, and no place is better than at the Lyric Theatre. But with all this, he makes as good grades as the others.
text:
"A cheerful disposition is a fund of ready capital."
"Tommie," with several of his classmates, has been taking a course in light dramatics during the year. Since Pharmacy, he will practice; he must know the whims of humanity, and no place is better than at the Lyric Theatre. But with all this, he makes as good grades as the others.

name: Slaughter, Thomas Gaines
motto: "Let me not live, if I had not rather hear thee discourse than see a play."
description: Slaughter has proven a good student, and a man with conscientious ability, as the secretary of the Pharmaceutical Association. Would that all men were as true to their friends as he, and would that all men were as anxious to do their duty, correctly.
text:
"Let me not live, if I had not rather hear thee discourse than see a play."
Slaughter has proven a good student, and a man with conscientious ability, as the secretary of the Pharmaceutical Association. Would that all men were as true to their friends as he, and would that all men were as anxious to do their duty, correctly.

name: Condon, William James
motto: "Nothing can cover his high fame but heaven."
description: Condon comes from the metropolis of Winona, where we spend weeks between trains. His ambition is to possess a chain of soda fountains in his native city—in his profession we wish him the best of success.
text:
"Nothing can cover his high fame but heaven."
Condon comes from the metropolis of Winona, where we spend weeks between trains. His ambition is to possess a chain of soda fountains in his native city—in his profession we wish him the best of success.

name: Woodsville, William Henry Catchings
motto: "His bark is worse than his bite."
description: Bill, the freshest of freshmen, was a terror to the upperclassmen in Taylor Hall. Aside from his pranks, Bill is a clean, manly fellow, and many are the friends he can claim on the campus.
text:
"His bark is worse than his bite."
Bill, the freshest of freshmen, was a terror to the upperclassmen in Taylor Hall. Aside from his pranks, Bill is a clean, manly fellow, and many are the friends he can claim on the campus.

name: Blue Mountain, James Dunham
motto: "Knowledge is proud that he has learned so much."
description: James! why do you think so much? He is never heard in the crowd, but ask his professors who speaks the loudest on a written quiz.
text:
"Knowledge is proud that he has learned so much."
James! why do you think so much? He is never heard in the crowd, but ask his professors who speaks the loudest on a written quiz.

name: New Albany, Frank G. Caldwell
motto: "I would rather make tetramethyl diamidobenzophenone chloride than to eat."
description: Frank has been a hard working student, and with his character and scientific ability, a success is assured. We wonder what the inexperienced pharmacists will do without his guidance.
text:
"I would rather make tetramethyl diamidobenzophenone chloride than to eat."
Frank has been a hard working student, and with his character and scientific ability, a success is assured. We wonder what the inexperienced pharmacists will do without his guidance.

name: Collins, Archie Carr Ramsay
motto: "Whatever he did was done with so much ease, In him alone 'twas natural to please."
description: Archie is a consistent student, an athlete, and a leader in social life. Much do we owe him for the success of the Cotillion Club.
text:
"Whatever he did was done with so much ease, In him alone 'twas natural to please."
Archie is a consistent student, an athlete, and a leader in social life. Much do we owe him for the success of the Cotillion Club.

name: Lockard, L. L.
motto: "He was ever precise in promise keeping."
description: Lockard is truly said to be one of the brainiest, most level-headed men on the campus. A debater of ability, and a literary man of attainments—he has made his mark here, as he will do in the larger sphere that awaits him.
text:
"He was ever precise in promise keeping."
Lockard is truly said to be one of the brainiest, most level-headed men on the campus. A debater of ability, and a literary man of attainments—he has made his mark here, as he will do in the larger sphere that awaits him.

name: Anderson, Harry Ralph
motto: "O, it is excellent to have a giant's strength."
description: "Horse" looks like a hopeless book-worm, but he isn't. The scholarship side of his character is well developed, but he bears it very well; indeed, he is a fine fellow, well liked by all who know him.
text:
"O, it is excellent to have a giant's strength."
"Horse" looks like a hopeless book-worm, but he isn't. The scholarship side of his character is well developed, but he bears it very well; indeed, he is a fine fellow, well liked by all who know him.

name: Lindsey, Anne Clyde
motto: "They're only truly great who are truly good."
description: Never has there been a more conscientious student at "Ole Miss" than Clyde. She stands high in the hearts of her fellow students as well as in her classes.
text:
"They're only truly great who are truly good."
Never has there been a more conscientious student at "Ole Miss" than Clyde. She stands high in the hearts of her fellow students as well as in her classes.

name: Stamper, Henry Grady
motto: "Charms strike the sight, but merit wins the soul."
description: A man from Decatur yelept Henry Grady, whose oratory has made the Phi Sigma hall resound over and over. He knows not much Greek, nor is he a shark in Latin, but for an all-round man, show us a better. The world looks on and says, "this is a man."
text:
"Charms strike the sight, but merit wins the soul."
A man from Decatur yelept Henry Grady, whose oratory has made the Phi Sigma hall resound over and over. He knows not much Greek, nor is he a shark in Latin, but for an all-round man, show us a better. The world looks on and says, "this is a man."

name: Butler, Thomas Bentley Jr.
motto: "You have too much respect upon the world. They lose it that do buy it with much care."
description: This fellow is as conscientious as any man ever dared to be. "T. B." is no athlete, nor does he lay claim to any athletic honors, but his diploma will be marked "with distinction." A person capable of fooling two profs into taking him as an assistant shows that he is above the average in literary attainments.
text:
"You have too much respect upon the world. They lose it that do buy it with much care."
This fellow is as conscientious as any man ever dared to be. "T. B." is no athlete, nor does he lay claim to any athletic honors, but his diploma will be marked "with distinction." A person capable of fooling two profs into taking him as an assistant shows that he is above the average in literary attainments.

name: Mclean, Ellen
motto: "Her Angelo face, As the great eye of heaven, shined bright, And made a sunshine in the shady place."
description: Ellen has added honesty, frankness, sweetness and faithfulness to the knowledge, gained from books, and this combination makes her a sincere type. Our only regret is that we had her with us only two years, for in her we found a real girl—one who struck a happy medium between work and play.
text:
"Her Angelo face, As the great eye of heaven, shined bright, And made a sunshine in the shady place."
Ellen has added honesty, frankness, sweetness and faithfulness to the knowledge, gained from books, and this combination makes her a sincere type. Our only regret is that we had her with us only two years, for in her we found a real girl—one who struck a happy medium between work and play.

name: Jackson, Forrest Butler
motto: "Let the world slide, let the world go; A fig for care, and a fig for woe."
clubs: Hermaean Critic '21, Anniversary Speaker '22, Vice-President '22, President '23
Intercollegiate Debater with S. M. U. at Dallas, '22
Executive Council A. S. B. O., '23
description: An all-round man with the ability to turn out more work in less time than the best of the rest of the world. He takes his troubles as they come, never anticipates, never worries, and the result is a good-natured character that is both contagious and magnetic. He won't let me say more.
text:
"Let the world slide, let the world go; A fig for care, and a fig for woe."
An all-round man with the ability to turn out more work in less time than the best of the rest of the world. He takes his troubles as they come, never anticipates, never worries, and the result is a good-natured character that is both contagious and magnetic. He won't let me say more.

name: Price, Myrtle Lucile
motto: "She moves a goddess, and she looks a queen."
description: Indeed, Ole Miss is blessed in gaining such a co-ed as Myrtle, bonny and blythe, good and gay. Ever ready for fun and frolic, but also capable of holding many responsibilities, honors, and positions in which only ability will serve.
text:
"She moves a goddess, and she looks a queen."
Indeed, Ole Miss is blessed in gaining such a co-ed as Myrtle, bonny and blythe, good and gay. Ever ready for fun and frolic, but also capable of holding many responsibilities, honors, and positions in which only ability will serve.

name: Simon, Rosenthal
motto: "A decent boldness ever meets with friends."
description: "Bolshe" has meant much to the success of this annual, by selling and collecting, in both arts he excels. You can see that he has stuck by the Scrubs in baseball and will be a good prospect for the regular team this year.
text:
"A decent boldness ever meets with friends."
"Bolshe" has meant much to the success of this annual, by selling and collecting, in both arts he excels. You can see that he has stuck by the Scrubs in baseball and will be a good prospect for the regular team this year.

name: Tull, Willeen
motto: "Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all."
description: Willeen is not only a poet, but she is an actor, with every quality of a good student. She has received a degree in three years, and taught one year. How she did it is a mystery, but you know how our profs love the ladies!
text:
"Many daughters have done virtuously, but thou excellest them all."
Willeen is not only a poet, but she is an actor, with every quality of a good student. She has received a degree in three years, and taught one year. How she did it is a mystery, but you know how our profs love the ladies!

name: Bell, James Warsaw, Jr.
motto: "Never so busy a man as he ther was, And yet he seemed busier than he was."
description: He has never lost an argument! His career began with the Third Floor News. "Sannie" has lived on the campus since "the knee high to a kitten age," knowing nothing else than to give his all to Ole Miss. The best of pals that any man ever had, one you won't forget!
text:
"Never so busy a man as he ther was, And yet he seemed busier than he was."
He has never lost an argument! His career began with the Third Floor News. "Sannie" has lived on the campus since "the knee high to a kitten age," knowing nothing else than to give his all to Ole Miss. The best of pals that any man ever had, one you won't forget!

name: Lewis, Arthur B.
motto: "It is the mind that makes the man, Our vigor is our immortal soul."
description: Arthur is of the type that do not happen often. He inspires you with a feeling of confidence not expressed by words, but by the magnetism of his character. Would that we were all as energetic, unassuming, with a depth of soul unsounded!
text:
"It is the mind that makes the man, Our vigor is our immortal soul."
Arthur is of the type that do not happen often. He inspires you with a feeling of confidence not expressed by words, but by the magnetism of his character. Would that we were all as energetic, unassuming, with a depth of soul unsounded!

name: Loggins, Teresa Camp
motto: "There is none like her—none, Nor will be when all summers have ceased."
description: She is delightful in conversation and her charm adds to her pleasing personality. A rare combination of wit, humor, and sensibility with the quality of looking on the bright side of life.
text:
"There is none like her—none, Nor will be when all summers have ceased."
She is delightful in conversation and her charm adds to her pleasing personality. A rare combination of wit, humor, and sensibility with the quality of looking on the bright side of life.

name: Crymes, Robert Jones
motto: "Life is just one blooming thing after another."
description: The familiar figure of "Coach" inspires us with a vague sense of adventure. Hobo, sailor, man of the world, student, and would-be athlete; all these roles. Robert fills to perfection, plus that of a "really good fellow" when you know him.
text:
"Life is just one blooming thing after another."
The familiar figure of "Coach" inspires us with a vague sense of adventure. Hobo, sailor, man of the world, student, and would-be athlete; all these roles. Robert fills to perfection, plus that of a "really good fellow" when you know him.

name: Bailey, Beatrice
motto: "For knowledge is of things we see; And yet we trust it comes from thee, a beam in darkness."
description: A "Latin Shark" in the true sense of the word is BeBe, always ready for fun, but studious, as one could easily tell by her good grades.
text:
"For knowledge is of things we see; And yet we trust it comes from thee, a beam in darkness."
A "Latin Shark" in the true sense of the word is BeBe, always ready for fun, but studious, as one could easily tell by her good grades.

name: McGowen, Hardy R.
motto: "There is no road or ready way to virtue."
description: Musician, wireless operator, and student, Mac gives us all that feeling of, "Why can't I do it." A hard worker, dependable and trustworthy, and a mighty fine fellow.
text:
"There is no road or ready way to virtue."
Musician, wireless operator, and student, Mac gives us all that feeling of, "Why can't I do it." A hard worker, dependable and trustworthy, and a mighty fine fellow.

name: Roberson, Gladys Ray
motto: "They are never alone that are accompanied with noble thoughts."
description: Gladys Ray was one of the friendliest of the friendly, and a good addition to any social gathering—she is a gay symbol of earnestness and courage.
text:
"They are never alone that are accompanied with noble thoughts."
Gladys Ray was one of the friendliest of the friendly, and a good addition to any social gathering—she is a gay symbol of earnestness and courage.

name: Creekmore, Wade Hampton
motto: "Kind, true and gentle hearted, a simple, honest man; He works with quiet persistence, but never as hard as he can."
description: Wade is the kind of fellow that one likes to meet, to be with, and is sure to remember. We predict for him a brilliant career before the bar of the Old Delta State.
text:
"Kind, true and gentle hearted, a simple, honest man; He works with quiet persistence, but never as hard as he can."
Wade is the kind of fellow that one likes to meet, to be with, and is sure to remember. We predict for him a brilliant career before the bar of the Old Delta State.

name: Wade, Elizabeth
motto: "Wise to resolve, and patient to perform."
description: "Lib" is a favorite among all the girls and boys on the campus, and she is greatly liked for her gentle kindness and sweet disposition. "To know her is to love her."
text:
"Wise to resolve, and patient to perform."
"Lib" is a favorite among all the girls and boys on the campus, and she is greatly liked for her gentle kindness and sweet disposition. "To know her is to love her."

name: Yerger, Wirt Adams
motto: "I think him so, because I think him so."
description: The king of these who have set their dramatic wagons to a star of the first magnitude! "Wirt" made his debut in the theatrical world in "Stop Thief." We feel sure that he will be appearing on the first pages of the Billboard within another two years.
text:
"I think him so, because I think him so."
The king of these who have set their dramatic wagons to a star of the first magnitude! "Wirt" made his debut in the theatrical world in "Stop Thief." We feel sure that he will be appearing on the first pages of the Billboard within another two years.

name: Dickey, Norma Edna
motto: "The common mouth, So gross to express delight In praise of her grew oratory."
description: Norma is "fine"-because she is just Norma; and there is pure delight in knowing her. Noble and true, studious and full of college life is our dear, lovable "Physics Master" of Ward Hall.
text:
"The common mouth, So gross to express delight In praise of her grew oratory."
Norma is "fine"-because she is just Norma; and there is pure delight in knowing her. Noble and true, studious and full of college life is our dear, lovable "Physics Master" of Ward Hall.

name: Barbour, Calvin C., Jr.
motto: "Seas roll to waft me, suns to light me rise, My footstool earth, my canopy the skies."
clubs: Chairman Second Cotillion Club Dance Committee, '22
description: In Calvin we lose the one man who has done more than any other to put Ole Miss where she is. Throughout his brilliant career he has stood for clean sport, and it is his unbeatable spirit that is responsible for many of the "come backs" for which Ole Miss is famous. A man of sterling qualities throughout, admired, respected and loved by every man. We'll never forget his "Fight 'em, dad-burn it, fight 'em in there!" Now, he is wedded!
text:
"Seas roll to waft me, suns to light me rise, My footstool earth, my canopy the skies."
In Calvin we lose the one man who has done more than any other to put Ole Miss where she is. Throughout his brilliant career he has stood for clean sport, and it is his unbeatable spirit that is responsible for many of the "come backs" for which Ole Miss is famous. A man of sterling qualities throughout, admired, respected and loved by every man. We'll never forget his "Fight 'em, dad-burn it, fight 'em in there!" Now, he is wedded!

name: Burns, Josie Ava
motto: "There's in you all that we believe of heaven, Amazing brightness, purity and truth, Eternal joy and everlasting love."
description: O, woman, perfect woman—what more could express delight, praise and honor of such a genuine dependable, studious and likable girl. In "Joe," we find, at all times, those qualities possessed by a true friend.
text:
"There's in you all that we believe of heaven, Amazing brightness, purity and truth, Eternal joy and everlasting love."
O, woman, perfect woman—what more could express delight, praise and honor of such a genuine dependable, studious and likable girl. In "Joe," we find, at all times, those qualities possessed by a true friend.

name: Waits, Dewey Hilton
motto: "Never elated when one man's oppressed; Never dejected while another's bless'd."
description: Have you ever picked up a single book in the library that you did not see this name among those who have been reading? A more well-read man and a source of general information has never graduated from any university. With all his reading, and with all his thinking, he is a conservative, but perhaps, that is what some of those who would be radicals need, a little more reading and a large amount of original thinking.
text:
"Never elated when one man's oppressed; Never dejected while another's bless'd."
Have you ever picked up a single book in the library that you did not see this name among those who have been reading? A more well-read man and a source of general information has never graduated from any university. With all his reading, and with all his thinking, he is a conservative, but perhaps, that is what some of those who would be radicals need, a little more reading and a large amount of original thinking.

name: Waits, Steve Hobson
motto: "Wit that can creep, and pride that licks the dust."
description: The name is a misnomer. Never has he been a weight to any class, and a man who loves his brother more can not be seen on the campus. A real student can truly be said of this friend of those who would think.
text:
"Wit that can creep, and pride that licks the dust."
The name is a misnomer. Never has he been a weight to any class, and a man who loves his brother more can not be seen on the campus. A real student can truly be said of this friend of those who would think.

name: Barrett, Ashton C.
motto: "O Love! thou art the very good of evil, For, after all, I cannot call thee devil."
description: About once in four years there comes to school an all-round good fellow whom everyone seems proud to claim as a friend. During our little career, Ashton has filled this place. The boy went on dangerous ground when he took the direction of the "chow council," but everyone seems satisfied and all's well. "Ole Ashcan" is quite a jelly-bean, too, and it isn't untruthful to tell that he has caused many a feminine heart to flutter. Go to it, boy. We're all behind you.
text:
"O Love! thou art the very good of evil, For, after all, I cannot call thee devil."
About once in four years there comes to school an all-round good fellow whom everyone seems proud to claim as a friend. During our little career, Ashton has filled this place. The boy went on dangerous ground when he took the direction of the "chow council," but everyone seems satisfied and all's well. "Ole Ashcan" is quite a jelly-bean, too, and it isn't untruthful to tell that he has caused many a feminine heart to flutter. Go to it, boy. We're all behind you.

name: Cook, Emily Augusta
motto: "We understand her by her sight, Her pure and eloquent blood, Spoke in her cheeks and so distinctly wrought, That one might almost say her body thought."
description: Emily, always full of pep and brim full of happiness, made the campus ring with her laughter and cheered everyone with her kind words. She was a good student, too—just ask her about "Math"!
text:
"We understand her by her sight, Her pure and eloquent blood, Spoke in her cheeks and so distinctly wrought, That one might almost say her body thought."
Emily, always full of pep and brim full of happiness, made the campus ring with her laughter and cheered everyone with her kind words. She was a good student, too—just ask her about "Math"!

name: Cavett, Van Andrew
motto: "Say to all the world, 'This is a Man.'"
description: We all are familiar with "Sparrow" Cavett's Bird Club. When not out chasing the feathery warblers. Van is usually to be found in company of warblers who aren't feathery—they say he gets them from the coop—we don't know what that is; but you have to leave it to Van; he knows all about it.
text:
"Say to all the world, 'This is a Man.'"
We all are familiar with "Sparrow" Cavett's Bird Club. When not out chasing the feathery warblers. Van is usually to be found in company of warblers who aren't feathery—they say he gets them from the coop—we don't know what that is; but you have to leave it to Van; he knows all about it.

name: Taylor, Lillian Narcisse
motto: "The fairest garden in her looks, In her mind the wisest books."
description: Lillian is a typical university girl, and has broad enough interests to be a true co-ed. Her singing voice and ready smile bring sunshine into many lives.
text:
"The fairest garden in her looks, In her mind the wisest books."
Lillian is a typical university girl, and has broad enough interests to be a true co-ed. Her singing voice and ready smile bring sunshine into many lives.

name: Talmadge, L. Wilson
motto: "For he knew when he pleased, he could whistle them back."
description: The red-haired man with the large spectacles and a face radiating good nature. He intended to major in French, but "certain obstacles" lay in his way, so next year he undertakes medicine as a side light. He is sure to be one of the lights in whatever he attempts as a life work.
text:
"For he knew when he pleased, he could whistle them back."
The red-haired man with the large spectacles and a face radiating good nature. He intended to major in French, but "certain obstacles" lay in his way, so next year he undertakes medicine as a side light. He is sure to be one of the lights in whatever he attempts as a life work.

name: James, P. Walker
motto: "He simulated Andy Gump With like result."
description: "Frog" aspired to be Secretary of Gordon Hall Board of Control. Many are those who remember his last great campaign as an admirer of Andy Gump. The best wishes of the Student Body and the expectation that he will do many great and good things go with him as he leaves Ole Miss.
text:
"He simulated Andy Gump With like result."
"Frog" aspired to be Secretary of Gordon Hall Board of Control. Many are those who remember his last great campaign as an admirer of Andy Gump. The best wishes of the Student Body and the expectation that he will do many great and good things go with him as he leaves Ole Miss.

name: Glover, Otis Triplett
motto: "How far that little candle throws his beams."
description: "Trip" has specialized in Commerce and Business Administration. His grades show that his has been a marked success. What we lose the business world is sure to gain!
text:
"How far that little candle throws his beams."
"Trip" has specialized in Commerce and Business Administration. His grades show that his has been a marked success. What we lose the business world is sure to gain!

name: Durwood, W. Aiken
motto: "I have seen the day of wrong through the little hole of discretion."
description: Those who know him find that beneath his quiet and somewhat shy manner, he has a ready supply of wit and fun. Success has begun to knock—the financial world has acquired a prodigy!
text:
"I have seen the day of wrong through the little hole of discretion."
Those who know him find that beneath his quiet and somewhat shy manner, he has a ready supply of wit and fun. Success has begun to knock—the financial world has acquired a prodigy!

name: Wells, William Steven Jr.
motto: "Love seldom haunts the breast where learning lies."
description: "Will" served apprenticeship in the corps at old V. M. I., and then brought his dancing feet to Ole Miss, to astound us by his success with the Co-eds. After all, he's a good chap. and his general popularity is shown in his being placed in the responsible position of treasurer of the Cotillion Club.
text:
"Love seldom haunts the breast where learning lies."
"Will" served apprenticeship in the corps at old V. M. I., and then brought his dancing feet to Ole Miss, to astound us by his success with the Co-eds. After all, he's a good chap. and his general popularity is shown in his being placed in the responsible position of treasurer of the Cotillion Club.

name: Wiseman, Martha Ida
motto: "Those graceful acts. Those thousand deccencies That daily flow from all her words and actions."
description: "Jack" is known among the students as one of the finest, best, all-round, and dearest girls that the university ever welcomed. It is just such a character and such a student that our old institution is glad to call "her own."
text:
"Those graceful acts. Those thousand deccencies That daily flow from all her words and actions."
"Jack" is known among the students as one of the finest, best, all-round, and dearest girls that the university ever welcomed. It is just such a character and such a student that our old institution is glad to call "her own."

name: Scruggs, Arthur E.
motto: "Extremes in nature equal ends produce; In Scruggs they join to some mysterious use."
description: Old Scruggs, great, big-hearted, always smiling. ready to help a fellow all he can—he is universally liked. As captain of the "five," or as end on the "eleven" he is known as the hardest fighting man in the game—and he keeps the whole team fighting. when discouragement is about to creep in. It'll be many a day before we forget the appealing cry that's rung loud and clear over many a floor—"Fight 'em Scruggs."
text:
"Extremes in nature equal ends produce; In Scruggs they join to some mysterious use."
Old Scruggs, great, big-hearted, always smiling. ready to help a fellow all he can—he is universally liked. As captain of the "five," or as end on the "eleven" he is known as the hardest fighting man in the game—and he keeps the whole team fighting. when discouragement is about to creep in. It'll be many a day before we forget the appealing cry that's rung loud and clear over many a floor—"Fight 'em Scruggs."

name: Smith, Prentis Edward II
motto: "There's nothing ill can dwell in such a temple."
description: Prentiss hails from Millsaps; he decided to get a real education, so he breezed in among us last year. His unfailing good nature and ready smile won him immediate popularity.
text:
"There's nothing ill can dwell in such a temple."
Prentiss hails from Millsaps; he decided to get a real education, so he breezed in among us last year. His unfailing good nature and ready smile won him immediate popularity.

name: Gibson, John Ford
motto: "They say, best men are moulded out of faults."
description: You know him! A fine student and one of the upholders of Phi Sigma; behind everything for Ole Miss, 100 per cent; his loss will be keenly felt, but we wish him the best the future holds.
text:
"They say, best men are moulded out of faults."
You know him! A fine student and one of the upholders of Phi Sigma; behind everything for Ole Miss, 100 per cent; his loss will be keenly felt, but we wish him the best the future holds.

name: McElroy, Lily May
motto: "Like perfect music unto noble words."
description: Lily May, artist and musician, has her share of talents. Her jolly disposition and pleasant smile make her many friends.
text:
"Like perfect music unto noble words."
Lily May, artist and musician, has her share of talents. Her jolly disposition and pleasant smile make her many friends.

name: Lord, Richard Guy
motto: "Honor and shame from no condition rise, Act well your part, there all the honor lies."
description: A man of sterling character, firm purpose, and likeable disposition. He has made a good student and has the respect of the faculty as well as his fellow students. A life of usefulness awaits him.
text:
"Honor and shame from no condition rise, Act well your part, there all the honor lies."
A man of sterling character, firm purpose, and likeable disposition. He has made a good student and has the respect of the faculty as well as his fellow students. A life of usefulness awaits him.

name: Sims, Glenn
motto: "Grace was in all her steps, Heaven in her eyes, In every gesture dignity, and love."
clubs: Undergraduate Representative Y. W. C. A., '21, '22
Chairman Finance Committee Y. W. C. A., '22, '23
description: Frank, forward, and open minded is Glenn, a faithful student and a good college "sport." In her we find power, patience, duty, strength and reserve—qualities, which few have. As we grow to know her, we discover a wealth of fun, humor, good fellowship and ability—directed by one clear brain.
text:
"Grace was in all her steps, Heaven in her eyes, In every gesture dignity, and love."
Frank, forward, and open minded is Glenn, a faithful student and a good college "sport." In her we find power, patience, duty, strength and reserve—qualities, which few have. As we grow to know her, we discover a wealth of fun, humor, good fellowship and ability—directed by one clear brain.

name: Artz, Frank O'Ferrill Jr.
motto:
"From the crown of his head to the sole
Of his foot, he is all mirth."
description: We've always had a fine crowd from Vicksburg, and Frank is one of the best. Besides being a fine student, he shows great dramatic talent as pilot of the Marionettes, but his humor is the climax—it has been utilized by the Mississippian's joke column to great advantage, and look at the Scandals of '23!
text:
"From the crown of his head to the sole
Of his foot, he is all mirth."
We've always had a fine crowd from Vicksburg, and Frank is one of the best. Besides being a fine student, he shows great dramatic talent as pilot of the Marionettes, but his humor is the climax—it has been utilized by the Mississippian's joke column to great advantage, and look at the Scandals of '23!

name: Hoggatt, Eva Mae
motto: "Whose little body lodged a mighty mind."
description: Eva Mae was one who "worked while she worked, and played while she played"; a sincere student and an energetic leader in any undertaking.
text:
"Whose little body lodged a mighty mind."
Eva Mae was one who "worked while she worked, and played while she played"; a sincere student and an energetic leader in any undertaking.

name: Hardee, Robert Leonard
motto:
"The power, the beauty, and the majesty.
That have their haunts in date or piney mountain,
Or forest by slow streams, or pebbly spring
Are mine to seek eternally and paint."
clubs: Phi Sigma Secretary, Treasurer, Reporter, Anniversary Speaker
description: Hardee is an artist of no mean ability—the publicity of Central Gordon is due to his genius. A hard student, a deep and clear thinker of the conservative school, he is a man admired by many friends, and one who is sure to succeed.
text:
"The power, the beauty, and the majesty.
That have their haunts in date or piney mountain,
Or forest by slow streams, or pebbly spring
Are mine to seek eternally and paint."
Hardee is an artist of no mean ability—the publicity of Central Gordon is due to his genius. A hard student, a deep and clear thinker of the conservative school, he is a man admired by many friends, and one who is sure to succeed.

name: Lingle, Hughy Beutel
motto: "For gain, not glory, winged his raving flight."
clubs: Phi Sigma Secretary, Treasurer, Reporter, Anniversary Speaker
description: The student body showed their confidence in this one by electing him to the Gordon Hall Board of Control. He is one of the mainstays of Phi Sigma—and a fine boy, notwithstanding. He leaves many friends behind who wish him all of the best.
text:
"For gain, not glory, winged his raving flight."
The student body showed their confidence in this one by electing him to the Gordon Hall Board of Control. He is one of the mainstays of Phi Sigma—and a fine boy, notwithstanding. He leaves many friends behind who wish him all of the best.

name: Gregory, Roy A.
motto: "What good deed may I do for thee?"
description: Gregory is a superintendent of county education, a student, a graduate, a father, and the owner of a Ford. We had best not say any more for fear that we over rate one of the future Medics of our good old state.
text:
"What good deed may I do for thee?"
Gregory is a superintendent of county education, a student, a graduate, a father, and the owner of a Ford. We had best not say any more for fear that we over rate one of the future Medics of our good old state.

name: Newell, Benjamin Lafayette
motto: "The surest pledge of a deathless name, Is the silent homage of thoughts bespoken,"
description: Ben has the name of at least two of the founders of democracy. We believe that he will be ever as honest as Benjamin Franklin, ever as ardent for the rights of man as Lafayette. He was a green freshman, but all the rough edges have been worn away, and soon we will have the polished stone.
text:
"The surest pledge of a deathless name, Is the silent homage of thoughts bespoken,"
Ben has the name of at least two of the founders of democracy. We believe that he will be ever as honest as Benjamin Franklin, ever as ardent for the rights of man as Lafayette. He was a green freshman, but all the rough edges have been worn away, and soon we will have the polished stone.

name: Santos, Julia A.
motto: "His heart was as great as the world."
description: From far away Porto Rico comes Julio, "Esta fabulilia, salga bien o mal, mi ocuribo, Por casulidad," yes, it is true, that everyone knows that Julio is the best of fellows, a hard student, and a friend to those who befriend him. We wish him the best there is, and hope that his native land will be blessed by the famous Doctor Santos.
text:
"His heart was as great as the world."
From far away Porto Rico comes Julio, "Esta fabulilia, salga bien o mal, mi ocuribo, Por casulidad," yes, it is true, that everyone knows that Julio is the best of fellows, a hard student, and a friend to those who befriend him. We wish him the best there is, and hope that his native land will be blessed by the famous Doctor Santos.

name: Holland, Theo Lawrence
motto: "If I cannot find a way I will make one."
description: It would require much less space to mention his bad qualities and entirely too much to enumerate his good qualities. It is enough to say that he is a man, a friend, and a gentleman in every sense of the word. He is sure of success—if he doesn't marry too soon.
text:
"If I cannot find a way I will make one."
It would require much less space to mention his bad qualities and entirely too much to enumerate his good qualities. It is enough to say that he is a man, a friend, and a gentleman in every sense of the word. He is sure of success—if he doesn't marry too soon.

name: Denton, C. L.
motto: "All the world is daft but me and thee, And thee is a little bit daft."
description: Chink is one of our most promising—not so brilliant, but a hard worker, at medicine and otherwise. It is rumored that he is doing more studying this year than last; a certain one is absent, and he is better able to concentrate. He is one of the best known of the Meds, and to know him is to like him.
text:
"All the world is daft but me and thee, And thee is a little bit daft."
Chink is one of our most promising—not so brilliant, but a hard worker, at medicine and otherwise. It is rumored that he is doing more studying this year than last; a certain one is absent, and he is better able to concentrate. He is one of the best known of the Meds, and to know him is to like him.

name: Howell, John F.
motto: "I am Sir Oracle, And, when I ope my lips, let no dog bark."
description: A big man with a big voice, that wastes lots of our time, and might do a lot of damage if the owner hadn't such a good disposition. As a doctor, Frank won't astonish the world, but he will make a success, and we wish him the best of it.
text:
"I am Sir Oracle, And, when I ope my lips, let no dog bark."
A big man with a big voice, that wastes lots of our time, and might do a lot of damage if the owner hadn't such a good disposition. As a doctor, Frank won't astonish the world, but he will make a success, and we wish him the best of it.

name: Kelly, Norman
motto: "The bookful blockhead, ignorantly read, With loads of learning lumber in his head."
description: A little man with a big head, and one that holds "some" brains, to put it in the vernacular—they say he can quote Cunningham's fifteen hundred pages from memory and still eat a hearty meal! This is an age of brains and Red is sure to get his part of what the world has to offer.
text:
"The bookful blockhead, ignorantly read, With loads of learning lumber in his head."
A little man with a big head, and one that holds "some" brains, to put it in the vernacular—they say he can quote Cunningham's fifteen hundred pages from memory and still eat a hearty meal! This is an age of brains and Red is sure to get his part of what the world has to offer.

name: Robinson, Howard Denman
motto: "But sure the eye of time beholds no name, So blest as thine in all the rolls of fame."
description: Old "Santa" needs no words from us; the best would detract from him. For four years one of the most consistent stars of the gridiron; one of the best defensive fullbacks in the South. His fighting spirit won him the captaincy in '21; and his genuine spirit of good fellowship, the love of the student body. His loss will be a real one to Ole Miss—the ranks of medicine will feel their gain before many years.
text:
"But sure the eye of time beholds no name, So blest as thine in all the rolls of fame."
Old "Santa" needs no words from us; the best would detract from him. For four years one of the most consistent stars of the gridiron; one of the best defensive fullbacks in the South. His fighting spirit won him the captaincy in '21; and his genuine spirit of good fellowship, the love of the student body. His loss will be a real one to Ole Miss—the ranks of medicine will feel their gain before many years.

name: Brown, Glynne
motto: "Whether the charmer sinner it or saint it, If folly grow romantic, I must paint it."
description: Glynne never makes any noise. In fact, you would not know he was around, but for the fact that someone is wanting a little help on this subject and on that.
text:
"Whether the charmer sinner it or saint it, If folly grow romantic, I must paint it."
Glynne never makes any noise. In fact, you would not know he was around, but for the fact that someone is wanting a little help on this subject and on that.

name: Bryant, Kirby K.
motto: "O, what may man within him hide, Though devil on the outward side."
description: "Scrubby" is the best of friends to all men and not a few of the fair ones. He has quite a few experiences to relate of the days in Sam's Navy. The old pluck of the Navy Days is seen in his everyday work around the Medical Building; the "hitch" at both places being without a single mark on the wrong side of the book.
text:
"O, what may man within him hide, Though devil on the outward side."
"Scrubby" is the best of friends to all men and not a few of the fair ones. He has quite a few experiences to relate of the days in Sam's Navy. The old pluck of the Navy Days is seen in his everyday work around the Medical Building; the "hitch" at both places being without a single mark on the wrong side of the book.

name: Davison, Fred Baas
motto: "I never knew so young a body with so old a head."
description: Freddie is the kind we need more of, steady and dependable, always behind anything for the best interests of the school—his criticism, when it comes, is appreciated, for it is always considerate and constructive. We're going to miss the little fellow next year—we wish him the best of luck, and hope that his skyscrapers of the future won't blow over in the breeze!
text:
"I never knew so young a body with so old a head."
Freddie is the kind we need more of, steady and dependable, always behind anything for the best interests of the school—his criticism, when it comes, is appreciated, for it is always considerate and constructive. We're going to miss the little fellow next year—we wish him the best of luck, and hope that his skyscrapers of the future won't blow over in the breeze!

name: Wilson, John Alex
motto: "I had rather have a fool to make me merry than experience to make me sad."
description: His unfailing good humor and cheerful disposition have made him many friends, and his wide range of activities, social and scholastic, stamp him as a successful college man.
text:
"I had rather have a fool to make me merry than experience to make me sad."
His unfailing good humor and cheerful disposition have made him many friends, and his wide range of activities, social and scholastic, stamp him as a successful college man.

name: Maxwell, Ike Shelby
motto: "I am the very pink of Courtesy."
description: Who is the fellow that laughs so funny? Gee "whiz!" No, you show your ignorance. That is Ike. Yep, one of the best fellows on the campus. His genial disposition and attractive personality make him welcome wherever he goes. If there is ever another bridge built across the "Father of Waters" we feel sure Ike will build it.
text:
"I am the very pink of Courtesy."
Who is the fellow that laughs so funny? Gee "whiz!" No, you show your ignorance. That is Ike. Yep, one of the best fellows on the campus. His genial disposition and attractive personality make him welcome wherever he goes. If there is ever another bridge built across the "Father of Waters" we feel sure Ike will build it.

name: Moseley, W. Pratt
motto: "He is a great observer and he looks quite through the deeds of men."
description: "Pratt" says that he is thankful Spanish is not the only language offered at Ole Miss, because he would fail to get his share of A's and B's. Pratt is somewhat quiet, but to the friends who know him, he is a conscientious student, a loyal friend, and a true gentleman.
text:
"He is a great observer and he looks quite through the deeds of men."
"Pratt" says that he is thankful Spanish is not the only language offered at Ole Miss, because he would fail to get his share of A's and B's. Pratt is somewhat quiet, but to the friends who know him, he is a conscientious student, a loyal friend, and a true gentleman.

name: Reid, R. Williams
description: Look with us, gentle reader, into the dim and distant future. In a quiet and peaceful hamlet, far from the madding crowd, we see the re-incarnation of Rip Van Winkle, his long white beard stained with the tobacco juice. seated in front of the village store. He is the leading citizen and is pointed to with awe, as the king of all the alchemists. Come to earth again, boy, page Rip Williams.
text: Look with us, gentle reader, into the dim and distant future. In a quiet and peaceful hamlet, far from the madding crowd, we see the re-incarnation of Rip Van Winkle, his long white beard stained with the tobacco juice. seated in front of the village store. He is the leading citizen and is pointed to with awe, as the king of all the alchemists. Come to earth again, boy, page Rip Williams.

name: Thomas, Magee Berry
motto: "I have a soul that like an ample shield, Can take in all, and verge enough for more."
description: Berry is anything else except "but a 'doubting Thomas'" when it comes to his belief in the merits of his chosen profession. We are expecting big things from this smiling, good-natured piece of humanity.
text:
"I have a soul that like an ample shield, Can take in all, and verge enough for more."
Berry is anything else except "but a 'doubting Thomas'" when it comes to his belief in the merits of his chosen profession. We are expecting big things from this smiling, good-natured piece of humanity.

name: Herbert, L. Herrington
motto: "Experience joined with common sense, To mortals is a providence."
description: Herrington came to use from A. & M. College. He was ineligible for basketball last year, but great hopes are entertained for him this season. His immediate popularity is attested by the fact that he was elected baseball manager this season. He is one of the Pharmachute's hopefuls.
text:
"Experience joined with common sense, To mortals is a providence."
Herrington came to use from A. & M. College. He was ineligible for basketball last year, but great hopes are entertained for him this season. His immediate popularity is attested by the fact that he was elected baseball manager this season. He is one of the Pharmachute's hopefuls.

name: Henry, Alton Wilkinson
motto: "Shall I, wasting in despair, Die because a woman's fair?"
description: One of the many who are devotees at the shrine of the Cinematograph! "Pie Face" is always serious and never smiles at any one. He is the sort of fellow who is examined every week for weakness of the eyes, because of hard study. People don't have to be such students to amount to something in this world, just a smile goes a long way!
